Quick Start Guide

Source: vignettes/quick-start.Rmd
quick-start.Rmd

CovariateSearcher Quick Start

This is a minimal vignette included in the package installation.

For complete documentation, see the online documentation.

📋 What You Need

1. Base Model

NONMEM control file with properly formatted parameters:

$THETA
; Simple names (NO TV_ prefix in THETA block!)
0.5 ; CL ; L/h ; RATIO
10  ; V  ; L   ; RATIO

$PK
; Use TV_ prefix HERE in PK block
TV_CL = THETA(1)
TV_V  = THETA(2)

CL = TV_CL * EXP(ETA(1))
V  = TV_V  * EXP(ETA(2))

$OMEGA BLOCK(2)
0.1 ; IIV_CL   ; ; LOG
0.1 ; IIV_CL_V ; ; RATIO
0.1 ; IIV_V    ; ; LOG

$SIGMA
0.1 ; RUV_PROP ; ; LOG

Parameter Format: value ; NAME ; units ; RATIO|LOG

Important: - In $THETA block: Use CL, V (simple names) OR 1_CL, 2_V (numbered) - In $PK/$PRED block: Use TV_CL, TV_V (with TV_ prefix) - OMEGA BLOCK: One value per line (not multiple values on same line) - Four fields required: value ; name ; units ; transform

❌ Wrong OMEGA format:

$OMEGA BLOCK(2)
0.1     ; IIV_CL   
0.1 0.1 ; IIV_V   ← Don't put multiple values per line!

✅ Correct OMEGA format:

$OMEGA BLOCK(2)
0.1 ; IIV_CL   ; ; LOG
0.1 ; IIV_CL_V ; ; RATIO
0.1 ; IIV_V    ; ; LOG

2. Analysis Dataset

NONMEM dataset with covariates (CSV format)

3. Covariate Search Configuration

CSV file defining which covariates to test: link : data/derived/covariate_search.csv (optional)

PARAMETER COVARIATE STATUS FORMULA LEVELS REFERENCE TIME_DEPENDENT
CL WT con power NA NA FALSE
CL AGE con linear NA NA FALSE
CL SEX cat linear 0;1 1 FALSE
V WT con linear NA NA FALSE

Column Descriptions: - PARAMETER: Model parameter to test covariate on (e.g., CL, V, KA) - COVARIATE: Covariate column name from dataset - STATUS: “continuous” or “categorical” - FORMULA: “linear”, “power”, or “exponential” (for continuous covariates) - LEVELS: Number of categories (for categorical), NA otherwise - REFERENCE: Reference category value (for categorical), NA otherwise - TIME_DEPENDENT: TRUE if covariate changes over time, usually FALSE Note: The package automatically generates the cov_to_test column (e.g., beta_WT_CL) during initialization. You don’t need to include this column in your CSV file.

🚀 Basic Usage

Initialize Search 

library(CovariateSearcher)

# Initialize a new search
search_state <- initialize_covariate_search(
  base_model_path = "models/run1",
  data_file_path = "data/derived/analysis.csv",
  covariate_search_path = "data/derived/covariate_search.csv"
)

Run Stepwise Covariate Modeling 

# Run standard SCM (Forward → Backward)
results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run1",
  scm_type = "standard",
  starting_phase = "forward",
  full_scm = TRUE
)

Check Results 

# Final model
print(results$final_model)

# Final covariates
print(results$final_covariates)

# View complete database
View(results$search_state$search_database)

📊 Model Selection Criteria

Models are selected if they meet BOTH criteria:

  1. ΔOFV > threshold
    • Forward: ΔOFV > 3.84 (p < 0.05)
    • Backward: ΔOFV > 6.63 (p < 0.01)
  2. RSE < threshold (default: 50%)

⚙️ Common Options

Selective SCM (faster Forward → Backward) 

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run1",  
  scm_type = "selective",     # Only test significant covariates from step 1
  starting_phase = "forward", # Start with forward selection
  full_scm = TRUE
)

Full SCM Workflow (Backward → Forward → Backward) 

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run1",
  scm_type = "standard",        # Standard SCM
  starting_phase = "backward",  # Start with backward elimination
  full_scm = TRUE
)

Custom Thresholds 

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run1",
  forward_p_value = 0.01,    # More stringent (ΔOFV > 6.63)
  backward_p_value = 0.001,  # Very stringent (ΔOFV > 10.83)
  rse_threshold = 30,        # Stricter RSE requirement
  require_cov_step = TRUE    # Require successful covariance step
)

💾 Save and Resume

Save Progress 

# Save search state
save_search_state(search_state, "scm_state.rds")

Resume Interrupted Search 

# Load saved state
search_state <- load_search_state("scm_state.rds")

# Continue from where you left off
results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run25",  # Last completed model
  starting_phase = "backward"
)

⚠️ Common Issues

Base model validation fails

Problem: Parameter format incorrect

Solution: Ensure parameters follow format: value ; NAME ; units ; RATIO|LOG

High RSE rejecting good models

Problem: RSE threshold too strict

Solution: Increase threshold

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run1",
  rse_threshold = 100,        # More lenient
  require_cov_step = FALSE    # Skip covariance step check
)

📚 Next Steps

Visit the online documentation for:

  • User Guide: Complete reference with all options
  • Complete Workflow: Detailed step-by-step tutorial
  • Recovery System: Understanding automatic error handling
  • Troubleshooting: Solutions to common problems

Function Documentation 

?initialize_covariate_search
?run_automated_scm_testing
?validate_base_model_parameters
?save_search_state
?load_search_state

📊 Workflow Summary 

1. Prepare Files
   ├─ Base model (.ctl)
   ├─ Dataset (.csv)
   └─ Covariate config (.csv)
   
2. Initialize
   └─ initialize_covariate_search()
   
3. Run SCM
   └─ run_automated_scm_testing()
   
4. Check Results
   ├─ results$final_model
   ├─ results$final_covariates
   └─ View(search_state$search_database)

✅ Example Output

After successful SCM run:

🎉 AUTOMATED SCM TESTING COMPLETE!
======================================================================
Final model: run85
Final covariates: WT_CL, SEX_V
OFV improvement: -23.45

Models tested: 47
Models completed: 45
Models failed: 2
Models retried: 2

Forward steps: 3
Backward removals: 1
======================================================================

That’s it! CovariateSearcher handles the complex workflow automatically.

For detailed examples and advanced usage, see the complete documentation.
